item.title: Testing blog posts
item.description: This is a line This is another line.

item.title: About me
item.description: I am first year PhD student in the department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at Cornell University. I am currently working with Prof. Christopher J. Earls as a part of Earls Group at Cornell University, where I am broadly working on Physics-inspired Machine Learning methods. I really enjoy working on the intersection of math, physics, and programming. I completed my Integrated Masters in Technology in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Hyderabad in 2020 with Prof.

item.title: Contact
item.description: You can reach me by Mail or on Telegram

item.title: First snippet
item.description: This content is in snippets/first/index.md pwd

item.title: Projects
item.description: My research interests include Machine Learning, Gaussian Processes, and Computational Mechanics. I am also interested in algorithms and optimization. Research Projects Dynamic Explicit Finite Element package for crack modeling using Floating Node Method Master’s Thesis (under the guidance of Prof. Syed Nizamuddin Khaderi) IIT Hyderabad (May 2019 - Jul 2020) Numerical modelling of Multipoint forming using Abaqus Materials and Manufacturing Technology Lab, Boeing Research and Technology, Bangalore (June 2018 - July 2018)
